# linux_maplestory

> Gets MapleStory (Steam app `216150`) running on Linux under Proton by porting the
> known-working macOS Nexon Launcher + VC++ runtime into the Steam Proton prefix.
> This file is an actionable runbook for an LLM/agent to install, launch, and
> **keep retrying through crashes until the game client window actually opens.**

## Your objective

Apply the patch bundle, then launch MapleStory and confirm the game client window
opens. If it crashes, read the crash signature, apply the matching fix from the
decision tree below, and launch again. **Do not stop at "install completed" — the
task is done only when the MapleStory client window is open and alive.** Keep
retrying, applying a different fix each iteration, until it opens. If you exhaust
the decision tree, report exactly which signatures you saw and what you tried.

## Hard rules (do not violate)

- **Never run `MapleStory.exe` directly.** Steam must hand `nxsteam.exe` the Nexon
  launch ticket. Direct launch fails immediately. Always launch through Steam.
- **Never enable `PROTON_LOG` globally** (e.g. by writing `user_settings.py` in the
  Proton tool). Global logging applies to every game and trips Nexon Game Security.
  Use the **per-game** launch option `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` for diagnosis only.
- **Always test a clean launch first** (no `PROTON_LOG`, no `WINEDEBUG`). NGS has been
  seen to fast-fail (`Unhandled exception code c0000409`) in an *instrumented*
  environment — logging can be the trigger, not the bug. Enable logging only to
  diagnose an already-broken clean launch.
- **Don't ship extra DLLs.** The game self-installs its own VC++ and legacy DirectX
  redistributables into the prefix on first launch (visible as `dd_vcredist_*.log`).
  The bundle ships only the VC++ runtime override (incl. `vcruntime140_threads.dll`).
- **The installer modifies the GE-Proton11-1 tool in place.** `apply_wine_patches()` backs up and replaces three Wine binaries in `compatibilitytools.d/GE-Proton11-1/files/lib/wine/` — `x86_64-windows/kernelbase.dll` (fixes the `0xc0000005`/Themida launch crash), `x86_64-unix/win32u.so` (force-success MapleStory's `SystemParametersInfo` sticky/filter-keys calls), and `x86_64-windows/dinput8.dll`. The patches are **build-specific to GE-Proton11-1**; on any other Proton the step skips with a warning and the launch crash returns — so **use GE-Proton11-1**.

## Prerequisites (verify before installing)

- Linux x64, Steam installed, logged in.
- MapleStory (app `216150`) **installed** and **launched once from Steam** so the
  prefix exists: `~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/216150/pfx/drive_c/`.
- **GE-Proton11-1** must be selected as the app's compatibility tool (Steam → MapleStory → Properties → Compatibility → GE-Proton11-1). This is required, not optional: the bundle's Wine binary patches target this exact build.
- A working Vulkan/graphics stack (see *Distro prerequisites* — a fresh Fedora/Debian
  box may be missing SELinux exceptions / 32-bit Vulkan / non-free firmware).
- `python3` is only needed if downloading/extracting the payload zip (not if `files/`
  is already present in the repo).

## Setup procedure

```bash
git clone <this repo> linux_maplestory && cd linux_maplestory
# Pre-flight: confirm the prefix exists (Steam must have launched the game once):
test -d ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/216150/pfx/drive_c || \
  echo "Launch MapleStory once from Steam first, then re-run."
./install.sh            # runtime DLLs + Nexon Launcher + registry patches + Wine binary patches to GE-Proton11-1
```

Key `install.sh` flags you will use while retrying:
- `--virtual-desktop` / `--desktop-size WxH` — for the `BadWindow`/`X_CreateWindow`
  crash or alt-tab input loss under XWayland (Hyprland, Mint, GNOME/mutter).
- `--kill` — stop any running MapleStory/Nexon helper processes before patching.
- `--skip-runtime` / `--skip-alt-tab` — apply only one half of the patches.
- `--launcher-source PATH` / `--mac-bottle PATH` — alternative Nexon Launcher sources.
- `-h` — full flag list. (There is intentionally no `--install-proton-settings`.)

## The retry loop

Repeat until the client window is open and alive:

1. **Launch through Steam** (GUI Play, or): `steam -applaunch 216150` (best effort;
   the GUI "Play" button is authoritative).
2. **Wait ~20–30s**, then **verify the client opened**:
   - `pgrep -af -i 'MapleStory.exe'` returns a live process, OR
   - the MapleStory game window is visible, OR
   - the Nexon connector log reached `gamelaunch` and did **not** then log
     `launch closed: <code>`.
3. **If the client is open → done.** Stop and report success.
4. **If it crashed → diagnose** using the decision tree, apply the fix, then go to 1.
   Capture the signature from `~/steam-216150.log` **only if a clean (no-log) launch
   already failed** — and re-test clean afterward.

## Crash decision tree (apply one fix per retry)

Read the crash signature (terminal output / `~/steam-216150.log` / connector log):

| Signature | Cause | Fix to apply, then retry |
|---|---|---|
| `X Error of failed request: BadWindow ... X_CreateWindow` | XWayland window churn | `./install.sh --virtual-desktop` |
| `0xc0000005` access violation at launch / Themida | Wine kernelbase bug | fixed automatically by the Wine binary patches; if it recurs, confirm GE-Proton11-1 is selected and the installer logged `patched: .../kernelbase.dll` |
| `Unhandled exception ... c0000409`, **no** `BadWindow` | Nexon Game Security | (1) ensure a **clean** launch (no PROTON_LOG); (2) switch Proton to GE-Proton10-x or stable Proton; (3) Fedora → try `umip=off` kernel param |
| DLL/module load failure, `Module not found` | redist not yet self-installed / bad launcher | launch via the Nexon Launcher (not the exe); re-run `./install.sh` |
| `.reg` reported failed by installer | protonfixes skipped it | import the `.reg` via **Protontricks** for app `216150` |
| Permission denied / AVC `denied { execmem }` | SELinux (Fedora) | `sudo setenforce 0` to test; permanent via `audit2allow` |
| `Failed to create Vulkan instance` / black screen | missing 32-bit Vulkan / driver | Debian/Ubuntu: `sudo dpkg --add-architecture i386 && sudo apt install libvulkan1:i386` + matching `:i386` driver; enable `non-free-firmware` |
| `ntsync` only as a missing-module log line | old kernel (<6.14) | benign fallback; try a backports/newer kernel if other fixes fail |
| In-game skill keys (`Q`/`W`/`E`/`R`, `Alt+1`–`Alt+5`) don't register | DirectInput not configured | `04-input-fixes.reg` + `05-appdefaults-winver.reg` apply by default; re-run `./install.sh` and confirm the imports landed |

Order of operations when unsure: **clean launch → `--virtual-desktop` → different
Proton → distro prerequisite → Protontricks**. Apply one change per iteration so you
know what fixed it.

## Distro prerequisites (the bundle assumes these are satisfied)

The reference setup is CachyOS (KDE Wayland), which ships these by default. A fresh
install on another distro can crash for reasons unrelated to this bundle:

- **Fedora:** SELinux is enforcing by default → `sudo setenforce 0` test, then
  `audit2allow` for a permanent rule.
- **Debian/Ubuntu:** 32-bit Vulkan + multiarch: `dpkg --add-architecture i386`,
  `libvulkan1:i386`, matching `:i386` driver; enable `non-free-firmware`/`non-free`.
- **Older stock kernels:** `ntsync` needs kernel **6.14+** (Debian 13 = 6.12, 12 = 6.1);
  GE-Proton falls back to fsync/esync otherwise.
- **GNOME on Wayland** (Fedora/Debian default): another XWayland compositor; use
  `--virtual-desktop` for the `BadWindow`/alt-tab case.

## Key paths

- Repo / bundle root: `./` — `install.sh`, `patches/`, `files/` (VC++ runtime + Nexon Launcher payload + `wine_patches/`), `README.md`.
- Prefix: `~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/216150/pfx`
- Proton log (diagnostic, opt-in per-game): `~/steam-216150.log`
- Nexon connector log: `…/MapleStory/steam-connector.log` (look for `gamelaunch`
  followed by `launch closed: <code>` or success).
- Proton tool: `~/.local/share/Steam/compatibilitytools.d/GE-Proton11-1/`
- Patched Wine binaries (copied into the tool by the installer): `files/wine_patches/files/lib/wine/{x86_64-windows/{kernelbase.dll,dinput8.dll},x86_64-unix/win32u.so}`

## Reference

- `README.md` — full setup guide, Troubleshooting, Distro-specific prerequisites,
  Collecting logs, Rollback. Read it for anything not covered inline here.
